m_Item1.QuestionText: In a row of 5 people, Alan is sitting to the left of Bob. Cathy is sitting to the right of Bob. Dana is sitting to the right of Alan. Emma is sitting to the right of Dana. Who is sitting in the middle of the row?
m_Item1.Explanation: The seating arrangement is as follows: Alan, Bob, Dana, Emma, Cathy. By placing Alan to the left of Bob, and Dana sitting to the right of Alan, followed by Emma, the middle position is occupied by Dana.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Ten people are seated in two parallel rows containing five people each. In row 1, A, B, C, D, and E are seated and all of them are facing south. In row 2, P, Q, R, S, and T are seated and all of them are facing north. Which of the following is definitely true if D is second to the left of B, who faces the person sitting second to the right of S?

Options.OptionText: E is the immediate neighbor of D
T sits exactly in the middle
R is an immediate neighbor of P

m_Item1.Explanation: Since D is second to the left of B and B must face the person second to the right of S, it implies C must face Q based on the limited positions possible.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Five people, V, W, X, Y, and Z, are sitting in a circle facing the center. W is second to the left of Z, who sits directly opposite V. X is not an immediate neighbor of W. Who is sitting third to the right of Y?
m_Item1.Explanation: If W is second to the left of Z and Z is directly opposite V, the seating must be Z, Y, X, W, V. Hence, W is third to the right of Y.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Seven friends P, Q, R, S, T, U, and V are sitting in a straight line facing north. T is third to the right of R. Q is to the immediate right of S. V is not an immediate neighbor of S. Which of the following pairs sits at the two ends of the row?
m_Item1.Explanation: Based on the constraints, if T is third to the right of R and Q is immediate right of S while avoiding neighboring V with S, the positions align to P and U at the row's ends.

m_Item1.QuestionText: There are five people - A, B, C, D, and E. They are seated around a round table facing the center. A is third to the left of B, and D is second to the right of A. Who is sitting opposite E?
m_Item1.Explanation: Placing A third to the left of B and D second to the right of A leads to the arrangement B, E, D, C, A, correctly positioning B opposite E.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a family, there are six members S, T, U, V, W, and X sitting in a row. V is not between U and X. S is to the left of X but not next to X. W is at one end of the row and to the right of T. Who is sitting in the middle?
m_Item1.Explanation: W must be at one end and to the right of T, with U and X positioned to avoid V being between them, resulting in V centrally positioned.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Seven students are sitting in a row. A is to the immediate left of B and to the immediate right of C. D is at the extreme left end of the row. E is second to the right of F who is between C and D. Who is exactly in the middle of the row?
m_Item1.Explanation: Given D is at the extreme left and F is between C and D, E taking the second right of F causes A to fall centrally in the sequence.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How many persons are seated between P and Q if seven persons are seated in a row and all are facing north, where Q is second to the right of R, who is third to the right of P?
m_Item1.Explanation: R being third to the right of P and with Q at R's second right requires them seated sequentially with three individuals interposing.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Eight people are sitting around a circular table. A and B are facing each other. C is not an immediate neighbor of either A or B, and D is to the immediate left of E. Where is E seated with respect to C?
m_Item1.Explanation: When considering B exactly opposite A and accounting D by E's immediate left placement, E becomes second to the left of C.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Five friends P, Q, R, S, and T are sitting in a line facing north. Q and R are sitting next to each other. P is to the left of R but not at the extreme left end. T is to the immediate left of S. Who is at the extreme right end of the line?
m_Item1.Explanation: P cannot take the leftmost spot with its adjacency condition, and T left of S requires S at the extreme right end.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a row of ten people, four people are not sitting at an even numbered position, and five people are not sitting at an odd numbered position. How many people are sitting at both even and odd numbered positions?
m_Item1.Explanation: Given mutual exclusion, there remains one person unaccounted for in each condition, summing to two people both occupying odd- and even-numbered ends.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Five people are sitting in a row facing north. H is not sitting at any of the ends of the row. There are two people between H and S, and three people between S and R. Who is definitely between H and R?
m_Item1.Explanation: Whether H positions between S or not and with imposed positions, S invariably rests between H and R.

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Nine persons A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I are watching a movie in a theatre sitting in a row facing East. B is at one end of the row. H is seated adjacent to both F and G. C is to the immediate right of D and at the third place to the right of E. A is immediate left of F. F is at the third place to the left of B. Find out:

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Who is at the other end of the row?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Write the explanation here

SubQuestions.QuestionText: What is the position of F with respect to D?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Write the explanation here

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Who sits in the middle?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Write the explanation here

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following is true?

Options.OptionText: H and I sit next to each other.
Number of people sitting between I and A and E and C are same.
There more people sitting between D and A than that sitting between H and E.
C sits next to I and A both.

SubQuestions.Explanation: Write the explanation here

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a room, there are 5 people. They are A, B, C, D, and E. All of them can see the whole room and everyone else in it. The house has a mirror but each person can only see their own reflection and nobody else's. No one is standing beside the mirror, except C, who is facing the mirror and can see A. If C is standing in front of the mirror and looking into it, who is standing directly behind them if E is standing to the right of C?
m_Item1.Explanation: Since C is facing the mirror and can see A, A must be directly behind C, allowing their reflection to be visible in the mirror. E, positioned to the right of C, leaves space directly behind C for D.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Three friends are standing in a row. Sarah is not in the middle but is standing next to Emma. Sam is at one end of the row. Who is standing in the middle?
m_Item1.Explanation: As Sarah is not in the middle, and Sam is at one end, the only configuration that works is Sam, Emma, Sarah.

m_Item1.QuestionText: There are five people sitting in a line facing north. Q is sitting to the immediate left of P and to the immediate right of R. S is sitting at the right end. If T is sitting between R and S, who is sitting at the left end?
m_Item1.Explanation: The arrangement from left to right is R, Q, P, T, S. Hence, R is at the left end.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a circular seating arrangement, P, Q, R, S, T, and U are sitting. Q is to the right of P. T and S are not sitting opposite each other. R is sitting between U and Q. Who is sitting directly opposite to R?
m_Item1.Explanation: The arrangement is P, Q, R, U, T, S (clockwise). T is directly opposite to R.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a row, W, X, Y, Z, and V are sitting. Z is not at any end. W is to the left of Y but to the right of V. X is sitting second from the left. Who is sitting at the right end?
m_Item1.Explanation: The arrangement from left to right is V, X, W, Z, Y. Hence, Y is at the right end.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Among five friends A, B, C, D, and E, seated in a row facing north, B is to the immediate right of A. C is at one of the ends. D is sitting to the immediate left of E. Who is sitting at the severe left?
m_Item1.Explanation: The arrangement from left to right is C, A, B, D, E. C is at the left end.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Seven people P, Q, R, S, T, U, and V are standing in a straight line. R is third to the left of T. Q is third to the right of R and second to the right of V. Who is between Q and R?
m_Item1.Explanation: The sequence is V, S, U, R, P, Q, T. U is between Q and R.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a group of 5 people A, B, C, D, and E, facing north, A is directly in front of B. C is at the rightmost end and D is to the left of E. If B is in the middle, who is immediately to the left of B?
m_Item1.Explanation: Arrangement from left to right is D, A, B, E, C. D is immediately to the left of B.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Five students sit in a row. M is sitting two places away from N, who is left of P. Q is immediately to the right of N. L is not sitting at any of the ends. Who is sitting at the extreme end?
m_Item1.Explanation: The arrangement from left to right is M, L, N, Q, P. P is at an extreme end.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Eight friends, A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H, are sitting in a circle facing the center. If A sits third to the left of B, and D sits second to the right of C, who is to the immediate left of H?
m_Item1.Explanation: Assuming B is in one position, A will be seated third to the left, establishing one part of the arrangement. By placing D second to the right of C, the sequence allows F to be positioned between G and H, thus placing F immediately left of H.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Six people M, N, O, P, Q, and R sit in a single row. N and Q are at the ends, while M always sits right next to R. Who sits directly in the middle?
m_Item1.Explanation: Since N and Q are at the ends, R and M must be side by side, making M-R a possible segment. With three slots left and the given arrangement, O takes the middle position: N-M-R-O-P-Q.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Four girls are sitting on a bench to be photographed. Kanchan is to the left of Sweety. Kaveri is to the right of Sweety. Jyoti is between Sweety and Kaveri. Who would be second from the left in the photograph?

m_Item1.QuestionContext:
Read below given information and answer the questionsfrom 28 to 30


V, U and T are seated in a circle facing the centre. A, B and C are also seated in the same circle but two of them are not facing the centre (facing opposite direction of the centre). V is second to the left of C. U is second to the right of A. B is third to the left of T. C is second to the right of T, A Is seated next to- V.


Now answer the below questions...

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Who is second to the C’s left?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The clockwise arrangement is U T A V B C
where B and C are facing oppposite to the centre.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which two among A, B, and C are facing opposite to the centre?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The clockwise arrangement is U T A V B C
where B and C are facing oppposite to the centre.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: In which of the following pairs, second one is immediately right to the first one?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The clockwise arrangement is U T A V B C
where B and C are facing oppposite to the centre.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Five children are sitting in a row. S is sitting next to P but not T.  K is sitting next to R    who is sitting on the extreme  left and T is not sitting next to K.  Who are sitting adjacent to S ?

m_Item1.QuestionContext:
There are 8 people, amongst whom, half are men and remaining are women, are sitting around a circular table facing the center. They are P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W.A man is not sitting next to another man and a woman is not sitting next to another woman.

W is husband of P, who sits third to the left of T. S sits second to the left of U. S doesn’t sit next to P or T. W and R sit next to each other. Q is not an immediate neighbor of her husband U.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following is true?

Options.OptionText: V sits exactly between U and W
T sits third to the right of V
V sits second to the right of Q

SubQuestions.Explanation:
The clockwise arrangement is P U V S Q T R W
W ,U, S, T are men
and P, V, Q, R are women
When we draw the arrangement, it is clear that, V sits second to the right of Q.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Who sits third to the right of W?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The clockwise arrangement is P U V S Q T R W
W ,U, S, T are men
​​​​​​​and P, V, Q, R are women
When we draw the arrangement, it is clear that, Q sits third to the right of W.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: How many people sit between R and U when counted in the anti-clockwise direction from R?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The clockwise arrangement is P U V S Q T R W
W ,U, S, T are men
​​​​​​​and P, V, Q, R are women
When we draw the arrangement, it is clear that, There are four people between R and U.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following is false?

Options.OptionText: U and Q sit opposite to each other
There is at least one couple who sits next to each other

SubQuestions.Explanation:
The clockwise arrangement is P U V S Q T R W
W ,U, S, T are men
​​​​​​​and P, V, Q, R are women
When we draw the arrangement, it is clear that, U and Q do not sit opposite to each other

SubQuestions.QuestionText: If all the persons are made to sit in alphabetical order in a clockwise direction, starting from P, the positions of how many (excluding P) will remain unchanged as compared to their original seating positions?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The clockwise arrangement is P U V S Q T R W
W ,U, S, T are men
​​​​​​​and P, V, Q, R are women
When we draw the arrangement, it is clear that, positions of S and W remain unchanged

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Six persons A, B, C, D, E, F are sitting in 2 rows, 3 in each row
E is not at the end of any row

B is the neighbor of F

Which of the following are sitting diagonally opposite to each other?
m_Item1.Explanation:
D    B    F
​​​​​​​
A    E    C

m_Item1.QuestionText:

2. S is not neighbour of P.

for arrangement which is the correct from following:
a. P is to the immediate right of Q

d. U is between W and S

m_Item1.QuestionText:

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Six friends P,Q,R,S,T, and U are sitting arround the hexagonal table each of one corner and are facing the center of the hexagonal. P is second to the left of U. Q is neighbour of R and S. T is second to the left of S.
Find Who are neighbour of P?
m_Item1.Explanation: The clock wise arrangement will be UTPRQS

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Five friends, Alice, Bob, Charlie, David, and Emily, are sitting in a row. We know the following:

Alice is not sitting next to Bob.
Charlie is not sitting on either end.
David is not sitting next to Emily.
Who must be sitting in the middle seat?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Alice is not sitting next to Bob: This eliminates the possibility of Alice being in the first or last seat (as Bob would be next to her).
Charlie is not sitting on either end: This means Charlie can only occupy the middle seat.
David is not sitting next to Emily: This doesn't restrict any specific seat for them yet.
Since Charlie is confirmed for the middle seat due to the second clue, we can arrange the remaining seats like this:

[Alice/Bob] - Charlie - [David/Emily]

As per the third clue, David and Emily cannot be next to each other. So, the only remaining arrangement that satisfies all the conditions is:

Alice - Charlie - David (or Emily on either end)

Therefore, Charlie must be sitting in the middle seat.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A,B,C,D and E are sitting on bench. A is sitting next to B , C is sitting next to D , D is not sitting with E who is on the left end of the bench . C is on the second position from the right . A is to the right of B and E. A and C are sitting together in which position A is sitting.

Options.OptionText: C is betwee A and B
C is next to B and A
B is sitting between C and A
A is sitting between B and C

m_Item1.Explanation:
The left to right arrangement 
​​​​​​​E B A C D

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following has the pair with the second person sitting to the immediate right of the first person?

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Directions for questions 17 to 20: Nine people, Archana, Bharat, Charita, Dinesh, Elina, Frank, Gautam, Harshad and Ishita are sitting in a row but not necessarily in the same order. There are three people sitting between Frank and Charita. Dinesh is sitting 7th to the right of Bharat. Gautam sitting on extreme right chair. Harshad and Archana are sitting next to each other and neither of them is a neighbor of Frank. Elina is sitting in the middle

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Who sits to the left of Ishita?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Let’s arrange according to clues.
Elina sits in the middle and Gautam is on extreme right. This gives: _ _ _ _ E _ _ _ G
Dinesh is 7th to the right of Bharat. This gives: B _ _ _ E _ _ D G
Now, as neither Harshad nor Archana sits next to Frank and there are 3 people between Frank and Charita, the only possibility is: B C H/A A/H E F I D G
Hence, answer is Frank.

SubQuestions.QuestionText:
How many people sit between Harshad and Gautam?
(A) 4            (B) 5        (C) 6         (D)
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Let’s arrange according to clues.
Elina sits in the middle and Gautam is on extreme right. This gives: _ _ _ _ E _ _ _ G
Dinesh is 7th to the right of Bharat. This gives: B _ _ _ E _ _ D G
Now, as neither Harshad nor Archana sits next to Frank and there are 3 people between Frank and Charita, the only possibility is: B C H/A A/H E F I D G
As position of Harshad is not fixed, we cannot determine the answer.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following options show people sitting next to each other?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Let’s arrange according to clues.
Elina sits in the middle and Gautam is on extreme right. This gives: _ _ _ _ E _ _ _ G
Dinesh is 7th to the right of Bharat. This gives: B _ _ _ E _ _ D G
Now, as neither Harshad nor Archana sits next to Frank and there are 3 people between Frank and Charita, the only possibility is: B C H/A A/H E F I D G
Hence, Dinesh and Ishita sit next to each other.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following is definitely false?

Options.OptionText: Harshad and Elina sit next to each other.
Number of people sitting between Bharat and Frank is equal to those sitting between Dinesh and Harshad.
There are 5 people sitting between Charita and Dinesh.
Ishita sits 2th to the right of Archana.

SubQuestions.Explanation:
Let’s arrange according to clues.
Elina sits in the middle and Gautam is on extreme right. This gives: _ _ _ _ E _ _ _ G
Dinesh is 7th to the right of Bharat. This gives: B _ _ _ E _ _ D G
Now, as neither Harshad nor Archana sits next to Frank and there are 3 people between Frank and Charita, the only possibility is: B C H/A A/H E F I D G
Options A, B and C are either true or may be true. Option D is definitely false.

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Directions for questions 16 to 20: There are eight people – A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H seating equidistant around a table and playing cards. B and C sit opposite to each other. There are 2 people sitting between F and D. A, who sits opposite to F, is on 3rd chair to the left of G. H is 3nd to the left of A.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Who sits next to F immediately on his right?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Look at the diagram. A, who sits opposite to F, is on 3rd chair to the left of G. H is 3nd to the left of A. So we get stage 1. As there are two people sitting between F and D, D will next to A. As B and C sit opposite to each other, they have only 1 pair of opposite chairs available as shown in stage 2 diagram. It also means, D and E are neighbors of A.  So the final arrangement with all possibilities will be seen in stage 2 diagram.
Hence, H sits to immediate right of F.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following is true?

Options.OptionText: G and B sit next to each other.
D and C sit next to each other.
D and E do not sit opposite to each other.
H and D sit opposite to each other.

SubQuestions.Explanation:
Look at the diagram. A, who sits opposite to F, is on 3rd chair to the left of G. H is 3nd to the left of A. So we get stage 1. As there are two people sitting between F and D, D will next to A. As B and C sit opposite to each other, they have only 1 pair of opposite chairs available as shown in stage 2 diagram. It also means, D and E are neighbors of A.  So the final arrangement with all possibilities will be seen in stage 2 diagram.
Option A, B and C are possibly true but not always. Hence, option C is true.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of following options list people who may sit adjacent to each other?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Look at the diagram. A, who sits opposite to F, is on 3rd chair to the left of G. H is 3nd to the left of A. So we get stage 1. As there are two people sitting between F and D, D will next to A. As B and C sit opposite to each other, they have only 1 pair of opposite chairs available as shown in stage 2 diagram. It also means, D and E are neighbors of A.  So the final arrangement with all possibilities will be seen in stage 2 diagram.
Hence, B, D and A can sit next to each other.

SubQuestions.QuestionText:
Who sits third to the left of D?
(A) F        (B)         (C)        (D)
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Look at the diagram. A, who sits opposite to F, is on 3rd chair to the left of G. H is 3nd to the left of A. So we get stage 1. As there are two people sitting between F and D, D will next to A. As B and C sit opposite to each other, they have only 1 pair of opposite chairs available as shown in stage 2 diagram. It also means, D and E are neighbors of A.  So the final arrangement with all possibilities will be seen in stage 2 diagram.
D can sit either to left of A or to right of A. So person sitting on 3rd to his left can be B or C or F.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: If only F and B interchange their positions, which of the following may be true?

Options.OptionText: G and B sit opposite to each other.
H and G will sit next to each other.
D and F sit next to each other.
A and B sit next to each other.

SubQuestions.Explanation:
Look at the diagram. A, who sits opposite to F, is on 3rd chair to the left of G. H is 3nd to the left of A. So we get stage 1. As there are two people sitting between F and D, D will next to A. As B and C sit opposite to each other, they have only 1 pair of opposite chairs available as shown in stage 2 diagram. It also means, D and E are neighbors of A.  So the final arrangement with all possibilities will be seen in stage 2 diagram.
When F and B interchange their positions, F will sit next to either D or E.

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Seven friends Pawan, Queen, Ronny, Sunny, Tina, Uday and Varun are standing in a straight line facing north but not necessarily in the same order. Uday stands second from the left end of the line. Only two persons stand between Uday and Tina. Only one person stands between Pawan and Ronny. Varun stands third to the left of Ronny. Neither Ronny nor Uday is an immediate neighbor of Sunny.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following is TRUE regarding Queen?

Options.OptionText: Pawan stands second to the left of Queen.
Sunny is one of the immediate neighbor of Queen.
Only one person stands between Queen and Tina.
Queen stands at one of the extreme ends of the line.

SubQuestions.Explanation:
The arrangement will be:
Varun, Uday, Queen,Ronny, , Tina, Pawan, ,Sunny

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Four of the following five form a group as per the given arrangement. Which of the following does not belong to that group?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The arrangement will be:
Varun, Uday, Queen,Ronny, , Tina, Pawan, ,Sunny

SubQuestions.QuestionText: What is the position of Varun with respect to Uday?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The arrangement will be:
Varun, Uday, Queen,Ronny, , Tina, Pawan, ,Sunny

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following pairs stand at the extreme ends of the line?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Arrange all of them in line and check.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following represents the position of Ronny from the left end of the line?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
The arrangement will be:
Varun, Uday, Queen,Ronny, , Tina, Pawan, ,Sunny
78676B1

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Five runners, A, B, C, D and E ran a race. C won it and D didn’t finish last. Number of runner/s between D and B and A and E was/were same.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: In how many ways, result of the race in terms of ranking can be published?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Two possibilities for D and B and A and E, hence 6 ways.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: If A finished second, which of the following must be true?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Final sequence of runners is C A D E B.


​​​​​​​Or  C A B E D.( not possible)

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Six people, A, B, C, D, E and F are sitting around a circle. Two of them are facing away from the center whereas others are facing towards the center. Both persons, who sit opposite to each other, either face towards the center or face away from the center. A and B sit opposite to each other. C, who is facing towards the center, is not neighbor of D and F. Both neighbors of F face towards the center.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following must be true?

Options.OptionText: Both A and B face away from the center.
C and F sit opposite to each other.
Both A and B face towards the center.

SubQuestions.Explanation: Draw the arrangements

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following must be true?

Options.OptionText: D and F are not neighbors.
C and D sit opposite to each other.
F sits between B and D.
E and D sit opposite to each other.

SubQuestions.Explanation: Draw the arrangements

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Seven people L, M, N, O, P, Q and R are sitting around a circle facing center. L is sitting 3rd to the left of M and 2nd to the right of Q. None of R, N and O are neighbors. P sits next to L.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following must be true?

Options.OptionText: Q sits third to the right of O.
P sits 4th to the left of Q.
O and R are neighbors of M.
P sits between L and N.

SubQuestions.Explanation: Write the arrangement.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: If O sits 2nd to the left of N, in how many ways we can form the arrangement?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Write the arrangement.
